Curious Case and an option to add your mid cart bonus like we spoke about earlier. So
this is your chance to kind of really back up your product's biggest claim with a
compelling and curiosity arousing case study and then to introduce that mid cart as a
bonus that further supports and syncs up with the outcomes produced by that case
study. So here is how it breaks down. You're gonna start off by reaffirming that big sales
argument around your launch. You'll acknowledge that they haven't fully bought in yet,
they're seeing this hidden buy right the second you said cart now open so acknowledge.
They're not fully there yet then briefly future pace, that immediate ownership
experience.
This is what really kind of grounds it in something they can feel and see and see
themselves doing. Then the call to action will introduce that case study without
revealing too much detail. You really kinda wanna bring more curiosity around it and
invite them to kind of click the root to see more what that's about. Build desire around
those outcomes. If the case study is a student or a user of your product that
experienced some awesome results, build desire around those outcomes and with
another call to action and if you do have a mid-card bonus that further supports those
outcomes, I would usually add it in the PS. So let's quickly dive in and see how this
would play out in an example. So you do wanna be very clear in your subject line in
pointing out that a bonus has been added, otherwise it's very easy for that to just kind
of be missed. So you do want to point that out.
By the way, disclaimer, this is a draft email so excuse any errors or inconsistencies with
things you may have seen before. This one actually didn't go out so an extra 160K from
this sneaky little sequence. Don't know about you but I'm getting kind of tired of
rambling on about email copy is the fastest and most powerful way to double, triple or
even 10X the sales from your sales funnel. Blah, blah, blah, do I believe it? Hell yeah, I've
had front row seat to those miracles for over a decade strong but more importantly, do
you believe it? Probably not. So here's where you affirm and just validate that their
skepticism is still there and make that okay. I mean, we've been talking about the power
of email for just over a week now so even if you kinda sorta sense it to be true, if you
really believed it you'd already be inside 10X launches, blazing through the videos,
downloading one of the twenty plus proven theme plates and firing off profit bomb
after profit bomb as quickly as your over caffeinated fingers and convert kit account
could handle. But you ain't, you're still here.
So clearly we have some work to do and this is where you're just future pacing that
immediate ownership experience, something they could realistically see themselves
doing. But instead of trying to hammer you with more ideas around why you're totally
missing out on the email marketing game and how in literally the same amount of time
you spent reading that 1800 word blog post about the latest rules changes Zucks and co.
rolled out to f'up your Facebook strategy, you could have quickly knocked out a sneaky
four part email sequence that brought in more extra revenue than Zucks will ever
reward you with. So this is us setting up that case study using a little bit of curiosity and
